channel.title: Morning Cybersecurity
channel.description: Delivered every Monday by 10 a.m., Weekly Cybersecurity examines the latest news in cybersecurity policy and politics.

item.title: Why the hantavirus misinfo playbook looks familiar
item.description: From claims of crisis actors on cruise ships to pushing a horse dewormer as a cure-all, influencers and conspiracy theorists are using the hantavirus outbreak to recycle Covid-era misinformation online.
item.dc:creator: By Dana Nickel

item.title: Will cyber make the Trump-Xi summit agenda?
item.description: Concerns of China-linked hacking campaigns targeting U.S. infrastructure and artificial intelligence are intensifying ahead of this week’s summit between President Donald Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping.
item.dc:creator: By Dana Nickel

item.title: Inside the FBI’s cybercrime crackdown
item.description: The FBI is zeroing in on North Korea, Iran and Russia as it steps up its efforts to tackle global cybercrime, with the agency’s top cyber official promising the agency is not letting up.
item.dc:creator: By Maggie Miller

item.title: A royal visit meets a modern threat landscape
item.description: Following a shooting at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner over the weekend, officials are enhancing security ahead of King Charles III’s visit and tracking cyber threats, such as disinformation and espionage.
item.dc:creator: By Dana Nickel

item.title: What happens if Section 702 goes dark
item.description: Congress is agonizing (again) over a soon-expiring controversial federal surveillance program, but its true deadline may not be as imminent as advertised.
item.dc:creator: By John Sakellariadis

item.title: Scammers are coming for your tax returns
item.description: Scammers are using AI tools to craft hyper-realistic phishing emails and deepfake impersonations, targeting both filers and the accountants who hold their data.
item.dc:creator: By Dana Nickel

item.title: Budget battle tests US cyber defenses
item.description: The White House’s proposed cybersecurity budget cuts put one of CISA’s core divisions on the chopping block.
item.dc:creator: By Dana Nickel

item.title: What the cyber world is watching after RSAC
item.description: From Iranian-linked cyber risks to dismantling cybercriminal networks, security leaders convened in San Francisco last week to map the rapidly evolving threat landscape.
item.dc:creator: By Dana Nickel
